business.headline: Insurance claim rejected? Reddit user shares how this family fought back and wonMost people stop fighting after an insurance company rejects a claim. One Reddit user, however, shared how a family challenged a rejection linked to a pre-existing condition, spotted an error, escalated the matter, and finally won.

Sensex, Nifty recover after 4-day slide even as rupee hits record lowThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 49.74 points, or 0.07%, to close at 74,608.98, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 33.05 points, or 0.14%, to settle at 23,412.60.

RBI Governor says petrol, diesel price hike likely if West Asia crisis drags onRBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra said India may have to raise petrol and diesel prices if the West Asia crisis persists. The remarks highlight growing pressure on inflation, fuel imports and state-run oil companies absorbing higher crude costs.

Gold duty hike explained: What happens to your purchases now?Gold prices were already high, and now a fresh duty hike has made them even steeper. With jewellery stores expected to revise prices in the coming days, one big question remains, i.e., how will this impact your next purchase?

Should you buy jewellery stocks now or wait after the customs duty hike?The customs duty hike on gold and silver has rattled jewellery stocks, with concerns rising over weaker demand and higher costs. With shares of major players turning volatile, investors are now asking a key question: is this a buying opportunity or a signal to wait?

Tata Power shares crashed over 6% today. Should you buy?Tata Power shares fell more than 6% after weaker-than-expected March quarter earnings and mixed brokerage calls. The reaction spotlighted valuation concerns even as the company's renewable and solar businesses continued to grow.

Customs duty rises on gold, silver: Will buying slow or stay strong?Gold and silver have turned costlier after the government raised customs duty. The move has immediately sparked questions in bullion markets. Will higher prices cool demand, or will buying continue to stay strong despite the hike?
Sensex opens 100 points higher, Nifty above 23,400; Asian Paints up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex was down 216.67 points to 74,342.57, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 68.50 points to 23,311.05 as of 9:47 am.

Gold jumps Rs 9,206, silver surges Rs 16,743 after import duty hikeGold futures on MCX jumped Rs 9,206, or 6%, to Rs 1,62,648 per 10 grams in early trade. Silver futures also rallied strongly, rising Rs 16,743, or 6%, to Rs 2,95,805 per kg as of 9 am.

Air India cuts flights to major global destinations as fuel costs surgeAir India has suspended and reduced several international services from June as fuel prices rise and airspace restrictions bite. The cuts underline growing pressure on long-haul operations, with the airline warning that more routes could turn unviable.

From Thailand to Dubai: How your next foreign trip could weaken the rupeeWhat does a Europe honeymoon, Thailand getaway or Dubai shopping spree have in common? Beyond memories and Instagram pictures, they all mean more dollars leaving India. Let's understand here.

Gold, silver imports to get costlier as govt hikes customs duty after PM's appealDays after Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged people to avoid buying gold for a year amid economic strain caused by the Iran war and pressure on foreign exchange reserves, the government raised import duties on gold and silver from 6 per cent to 15 per cent.

Rising oil prices will impact inflation and interest rates: Nilesh ShahIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Nilesh Shah, Managing Director at Kotak Mahindra Asset Management Company and a member of the Prime Minister's Economic Advisory Council, addressed the recent market downturn and the Prime Minister's call for austerity.

Dubai court acquits man in Dh1.6 million dispute, says failed deal isn't fraudDubai's criminal court acquitted a man accused in an investment-related dispute. The ruling said the case pointed to a civil business fallout, not proven criminal fraud.

Paytm UPI growing twice as fast as UPI industry, here's what's fuelling its comebackIn its Q4 FY26 earnings release filed last week, the company reported that consumer UPI gross transaction value (GTV) grew 46% year-on-year to Rs 5.5 lakh crore in the March quarter, more than double (2.2x) the industry's 21% growth rate.

India has 60 days of crude, no shortage anywhere, says Hardeep Singh PuriSpeaking at the CII Annual Business Summit 2026, Hardeep Singh Puri, said India's energy supplies remain secure despite geopolitical uncertainty and military tensions involving major oil-producing regions.

Retail inflation inches up to 3.48% in April, driven by rising food pricesData released by the government on Tuesday showed retail inflation, measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI), rose to 3.48% in April from 3.40% in March. The reading, however, came in lower than Reuters economists' expectations of 3.8%.

Uday Kotak warns consumers to 'prepare for the worst' amid US-Iran conflictSpeaking at the CII Annual Business Summit 2026, Kotak warned that rising energy prices linked to tensions in West Asia could soon begin hurting consumers and businesses, especially those already struggling with limited incomes.

Sensex ends 1,456 points lower, Nifty below 23,400; TCS, Infosys fall over 3%The BSE Sensex crashed 1,456.32 points or 1.92% to close at 74,558.96, while the NSE Nifty50 slumped 454.15 points or 1.91% to settle at 23,361.70.

Tourism body backs PM Modi's travel appeal, seeks more foreign touristsThe industry body said tourism can become a strong source of foreign exchange if the right steps are taken. It suggested easier visa rules, better flight connectivity and stronger global marketing to make India more attractive to international travellers.

entertainment.headline: Billie Eilish's 3D concert film swings between emotional honesty and excessRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Billie Eilish's new concert film Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in3D) pairs her intimate stage persona with James Cameron's expansive 3D style. The result is visually striking but less assured when it reaches for deeper intimacy.

Filmmaker Peter Jackson calls AI 'special effect', warns it will destroy the world Director Peter Jackson said at Cannes that he sees AI in film as just another special effect but warned it could destroy the world.

Jr NTR-Prashanth Neel film's first glimpse to be over 3 minutes long Trade buzz says the first glimpse of Jr NTR and Prashanth Neel's NTRNeel may run for more than three minutes when it drops at midnight on May 19.

Fame Gurukul nostalgia hits hard as Arijit Singh-Qazi Touqeer reunite in Jiaganj Singer Qazi Touqeer shared a video revisiting his journey with Arijit Singh, from their Fame Gurukul days to a recent visit to Jiaganj. The clip captures moments from scooty rides around the town to a friendly game of cricket, offering a nostalgic glimpse into their bond.

Ayushmann Khurrana: Pati Patni Aur Woh Do is a pati-verse, not infidelity Ayushmann Khurrana says Pati Patni Aur Woh Do does not glorify cheating and calls it a "comedy of errors". The actor also spoke about comparisons with Sanjeev Kumar and Kartik Aaryan, the film's "pati universe", and his collaboration with Sooraj Barjatya.

Sanjay Gupta explains Dhurandhar gamble, says nobody would bet on Ranveer-Aditya Sanjay Gupta said Aditya Dhar spent four years mounting Dhurandhar before Ranveer Singh and the Ambanis came on board. He also revealed that nobody in the industry was ready to bet on the film given its budget and the phase Ranveer was in.

Don't issue me challan: Rakesh Bedi's comic moment with traffic police goes viral Rakesh Bedi shared a playful moment with traffic police officers who recognised him from Dhurandhar at a signal. The candid exchange drew attention online and highlighted renewed appreciation for his recent roles.

Salaam-e-Ishq sequel in development after nearly 20 years? Here's what we know Nikkhil Advani is reportedly developing Salaam-e-Ishq 2 nearly two decades after the original romantic drama released in theatres. The sequel is expected to follow three couples navigating modern-day relationships and could reportedly launch a larger franchise.

Kamal Haasan proud of Vijay's journey, says he's no longer just an actor Kamal Haasan said Chief Minister Vijay should now be viewed through his public office, not his film career. The remark underscored his earlier support for Vijay after the hung Assembly verdict.

Talwiinder sparks outrage after performing with Pakistani singer Hasan Raheem Singer Talwiinder sparked intense online debate after joining Pakistani artist Hasan Raheem on stage in Toronto. The viral concert moments have now divided social media.

Anushka Sharma's comeback ad leaves fans surprised by her look. Watch Anushka Sharma appeared in a new advertisement that went viral after being shared on Reddit and Instagram. The clip has revived discussion around her on-screen return, with fans praising her looks.

Banger start: Director Shankar congratulates Vijay on becoming Tamil Nadu CM Director Shankar congratulated Vijay in an X post after he took oath as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister. He said that people's wish has come true with Vijay.

Alia Bhatt gives saree a Cannes twist in new ivory-umbrella look. Watch Alia Bhatt arrived at the Cannes Film Festival in an ivory saree-inspired ensemble and umbrella. A video of the appearance is being widely shared on social media as her latest ambassador outing draws attention.

Ram Charan-Janhvi Kapoor's Peddi gears up for grand trailer launch in Mumbai The makers of Peddi have announced a grand trailer launch event in Mumbai on May 18. The update adds to the buzz around Ram Charan and Janhvi Kapoor's film ahead of its June release.

Rakul Preet on Jackky Bhagnani's viral 'situationship' remark: Mistakes happen Rakul Preet Singh said her husband Jackky Bhagnani's 'situationship' remark was a joke taken out of context. She said the viral clip omitted context from the full conversation.

Karuppu director reacts to 'film not for social media people' remark after row RJ Balaji clarified that his Karuppu remark was aimed at preconceived viewing, not social media users. His response followed online criticism as he said audiences were free to review the film after release.

Did the Met Gala curse keep Kylie Jenner, Timothee Chalamet away? Kylie Jenner and Timothee Chalamet reportedly skipped the Met Gala 2026 over fears linked to the infamous 'Met Gala Curse'. Here's why the theory still fascinates fans.

Gullak actor Harsh Mayar reacts to backlash over Annu Mishra recast Harsh Mayar addressed viewers after the Gullak Season 5 announcement revealed a major cast change. His message acknowledged fan disappointment and stressed that the show's family-driven emotion would remain intact.

Athiradi trailer: Basil Joseph vs Tovino Thomas in Malayalam campus chaos The trailer of Athiradi sets up a college festival revival that throws Basil Joseph and Tovino Thomas into conflict. It also marks Arun Anirudhan's directorial debut while reuniting the Minnal Murali team in a new way.

general news.headline: Trump arrives in China for high-stakes talks with Xi JinpingUS President Donald Trump landed in the Chinese capital alongside an entourage that included Elon Musk and Jensen Huang.

PM Modi isn't asking you to stop spending, but to spend wiselyPrime Minister Narendra Modi asked Indians to cut fuel use, delay gold buys and avoid foreign travel. The Centre said the voluntary appeals were meant to protect foreign exchange reserves, not signal austerity.

CM Vijay walks back astrologer-as-OSD move after backlash from allies, criticsAfter facing the wrath of the alliance partners and rivals, CM Vijay decided to cancel the appointment of his astrologer as officer on special duty in his department.

Kerala CM impasse deepens: Posters, Delhi talks, Congress split exposedPosters in Wayanad have targeted the Gandhis, warning them of political backlash and invoking Rahul's 2019 Amethi defeat to suggest that Kerala could become the "next Amethi" if the High Command chooses KC Venugopal as Chief Minister.

How China makes the granddad of NEET leak-proofIndia's national medical entrance exam, NEET-UG, has been embroiled in paper leak and exam centre controversies. While 2.2 million aspirants take the NEET, over 13 million candidates sit for China's Gaokao examination. Here's how China secures Gaokao with security and strict punishment.

Vijay govt floor test reveals raging inferno that could ravage AIADMKTamil Nadu CM Vijay's TVK government won the numbers battle in the Assembly on Wednesday. But the trust vote, meant to put Vijay's numbers to test, might have triggered the biggest internal war All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam has faced in years.

Convoy cuts, metro rides, no Cannes: BJP ministers cut VIP optics, echo PM appealDays after PM Modi urged Indians to embrace Covid-era restraint, BJP-ruled states began sharply reducing the size of ministerial convoys. Several ministers also cancelled foreign trips. Here's a look at the measures announced by different states as part of the austerity drive.

Updates: Autopsy report shows cardiorespiratory collapse as cause of deathPrateek Yadav Death News Updates: Prateek Yadav, the son of late Samajwadi Party founder Mulayam Singh Yadav and half-brother of SP chief Akhilesh Yadav, died in Lucknow on Wednesday at the age of 38 after a prolonged illness. Prateek, who was also the husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav, was brought to Civil Hospital around 6 am, where doctors declared him dead. His postmortem at the KGMU mortuary has now been completed by a panel of doctors under videography surveillance and tight security arrangements. Akhilesh Yadav visited the postmortem house during the proceedings and met doctors, later describing Prateek as a "good person" focused on his business. Preparations are now underway to hand over the body to the family, following which it will be taken to his wife Aparna Yadav's residence in Lucknow. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the updates surrounding his death.

This Indian city is at high risk as conditions set for strong El NinoThe development puts Chennai at risk, conjuring memories of the 2015 floods, which devastated large parts of the city, during a similar strong El Nino phase.

Katju finds Macbeth's 3 witches in TMC, Mahua Moitra calls ex-judge secret SanghiA war of words has erupted between retired Supreme Court judge Markandey Katju and Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra. Katju likened Moitra and two other MPs to the three witches in Shakespeare's Macbeth. Moitra called him a "secret Sanghi" while Katju revived the cash-for-query scandal.

Son absconding in Pocso case, Union Minister Bandi Sanjay calls it extortion bidBandi Sanjay Kumar, the junior Minister for Home Affairs, said his son should be punished if found guilty in a Pocso case filed against him. The BJP leader from Telangana insisted his family was being targeted and maligned. Bandi Sai Bhageerath has been accused of "grooming and molesting" a minor, and is absconding.

Live: North-South performance gap widens this yearCBSE Board 12th Results Live Updates: The Central Board of Secondary Education, CBSE has declared the much-awaited CBSE Class 12 Result 2026 today. Over 18.5 lakh students who appeared for the board examinations can now access their marksheets online through the official CBSE portal, DigiLocker. CBSE continued its trend of announcing board examination results on May 13, marking the third consecutive year of result declaration on the same date.

The internet is finally waking up to the beauty of Odia cuisineOdia cuisine is one of the most underrated, but with time, it is slowly getting its due. The most recent acknowledgement came from industrialist Harsh Goenka.

How Vijay wrote the perfect trust vote script with little oppositionVijay won the Tamil Nadu floor test with 144 votes, drawing support beyond the TVK alliance. The result exposed splits within the AIADMK and highlighted his outreach to opposition parties.

Satellite pictures indicate more than one suspected Iranian aircraft at Pak BaseSatellite imagery reviewed by India Today indicates Pakistan is building new hangars at the Nur Khan airbase, likely to conceal military assets from satellite monitoring following Operation Sindoor.

NEET paper leak: Rs 30-lakh deal with Gurugram doc in focus, Rajasthan brothers arrestedInvestigators say two brothers from Rajasthan bought the NEET-UG paper from a Gurugram doctor before the exam. The case now points to how the paper was allegedly circulated further among aspirants.

A nath, hathphool and vintage corset saree defined Alia Bhatt's third Cannes outingAlia Bhatt's third appearance stood out as she arrived in an ivory corset saree and parasol that gave her Cannes run a more rooted, softer turn.

Live: Keep expenses minimal, reduce use of imported items, PM tells CabinetPM's forex-saving push Live: Prime Minister Narendra Modi made a rare public appeal for nation as the Iran war and disruption in the Strait of Hormuz triggered fears of an energy and import shock for India. Calling on states and citizens to voluntarily reduce consumption, the Prime Minister urged wider adoption of work-from-home, fewer non-essential trips, lower fuel use, and restraint in purchases of imported commodities like gold. The appeal has already begun to translate into action. The Centre raised import duty on gold and silver to 15 per cent, pushing prices higher as part of efforts to curb imports and protect foreign exchange reserves. The rupee has also come under pressure amid rising oil costs. The signal is being reinforced symbolically from the top. The PM has ordered a 50 per cent reduction in the size of his official convoy and directed greater use of electric vehicles. Several states, too, are beginning to respond, aligning with the Centre's call to conserve fuel and essential imports as the external crisis deepens. Follow the latest updates on IndiaToday.in
Gen Z employee quits on the spot with brutal message after boss humiliates herA Mumbai employee said she resigned after being publicly shouted at over extra hours and shared the experience online.

Gold duty hike explained: What happens to your purchases now?Gold prices were already high, and now a fresh duty hike has made them even steeper. With jewellery stores expected to revise prices in the coming days, one big question remains, i.e., how will this impact your next purchase?

Why Vijay-Trisha remain Tamil Cinema's most loved on-screen couple in recent yearsVijay and Trisha's on-screen journey is back in focus from Ghilli to GOAT. Their six-film arc, fifteen-year gap and reunion underline an enduring Tamil cinema pairing.

Prateek Yadav, Mulayam Singh's younger son and Akhilesh's half-brother, dies at 38Prateek Yadav was the son of the late Samajwadi Party founder Mulayam Singh Yadav and his second wife Sadhana Gupta. He had been unwell for some time. A few weeks ago, he was admitted to a hospital in Lucknow, where Akhilesh Yadav had also visited him.

PR Sreejesh blasts Hockey India, claims he was sacked for a foreign coachPR Sreejesh slammed Hockey India for his removal as head coach for the Indian U-21 hockey team. He alleges that the organisation wanted to appoint a foreigner for the job despite his success with the team.

Indian-origin-CEOs trick behind US investor's share-market jackpot?American billionaire investor Michael Milken once joked that whenever a US company appointed an Indian-born CEO, investors should "buy the stock". A 15-year backtest of the theory now claims such a hypothetical portfolio would have delivered annual compounded returns of around 30%, far more than that of the S&P 500.

sports.headline: England drop Crawley, Pope as Ben Stokes returns for New Zealand Tests Ben Stokes returns to lead England against New Zealand as Zak Crawley and Ollie Pope miss out. The squad signals a clear reset after the Ashes, with three uncapped players and key recalls.

RCB vs KKR LIVE: Kolkata aim to continue impressive run against Bengaluru RCB (Bengaluru) vs KKR (Kolkata) Live Score, IPL 2026: Royal Challengers will look to take a step closer to the playoffs when they face Kolkata Knight Riders on Wednesday in Raipur. The Knight Riders, on the other hand, will look to extend their winning streak and stay alive in the competition.

PR Sreejesh blasts Hockey India, claims he was sacked for a foreign coach PR Sreejesh slammed Hockey India for his removal as head coach for the Indian U-21 hockey team. He alleges that the organisation wanted to appoint a foreigner for the job despite his success with the team.

Jasprit Bumrah stays No.1 Test bowler, Gill and Yashasvi ranked among top 10 batters Jasprit Bumrah remains the top-ranked Test bowler in the latest ICC update, while Yashasvi Jaiswal and Shubman Gill stay inside the batting top 10. The rankings underline India's individual quality even as the side waits for its next WTC match after the home defeat to South Africa.

Karnataka all-rounder picked as injury replacement for CSK's Ramakrishna Ghosh Chennai Super Kings have signed Karnataka all-rounder Macneil Noronha as an injury replacement for Ramakrishna Ghosh as the five-time champions continue their push for an IPL 2026 play-off

Neymar's World Cup call based on fitness and form, not sentiment: Brazil's Ancelotti Brazil coach Carlo Ancelotti said Neymar's place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup squad will be decided purely on fitness and form rather than emotion or reputation, as the veteran forward fights to prove he can still meet the demands of elite international football.

AB de Villiers urges Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to test himself across formats AB de Villiers has urged Vaibhav Sooryavanshi not to remain only a T20 specialist batter. He says Test cricket will probe the teenager's game, temperament and resilience far more deeply.

Did Prabhsimran Singh shut down 10kg weight gain rumours? Insta post sparks buzz IPL 2026: Prabhsimran Singh's Instagram story sparked fresh buzz after the Punjab Kings opener appeared to respond to unverified social media rumours surrounding his fitness and the team's recent slump.

Asia's Top Trap Shooter Zoravar Singh dropped from India's Asian Games squad Zoravar Singh Sandhu has sought a review after being left out of India's proposed Asian Games trap squad. His omission puts NRAI's rigid selection policy under harsher scrutiny this week.

Messi doubles salary with new Inter Miami deal, set to earn 25 million dollars Lionel Messi's new Inter Miami contract lifts his base salary to $25 million and keeps him miles clear atop MLS. The figures also expose Miami's financial muscle, with the club's payroll well beyond every rival.

Jitesh Sharma needs to hold back and stop trying to prove a point: Ambati Rayudu RCB vs KKR: Ambati Rayudu has urged Jitesh Sharma to keep things simple as the RCB wicketkeeper-batter struggles for runs. The remarks come with Bengaluru chasing a play-off spot and hoping Jitesh regains form.

PV Sindhu, Kidambi Srikanth storm into round of 16; Ayush crashes outP. V. Sindhu and Kidambi Srikanth entered the round of 16 of the Thailand Open with straight-game wins on Wednesday, while Ayush Shetty crashed out in Bangkok. Devika Sihag also impressed by registering her first Super 500-level win.

After GT humbling, SRH captain Pat Cummins given hefty fine for slow over-ratePat Cummins was fined Rs. 12 lakh after Sunrisers Hyderabad maintained a slow over-rate against the Gujarat Titans. The sanction came on a night when SRH sank to their lowest-ever IPL total and heaviest defeat by runs.

Watch: CSK Anhsul Kamboj meets lookalike from CM Vijay's oath-taking eventAnshul Kamboj finally met the police officer whose resemblance to him went viral at Vijay's oath-taking ceremony. Policeman Kamboj went viral on the day after his doppleganger was spotted next to Vijay at the event.

Never trusted VAR: Guardiola blasts referees as Man City title race intensifiesManchester City manager Pep Guardiola compared the Video Assistant Referee (VAR) system to the flip of a coin on Tuesday and said his side must perform well enough to avoid being affected by controversial decisions in the Premier League title race.

What went wrong with Mumbai Indians in IPL 2026?Mumbai Indians' IPL 2026 season fell apart under the weight of poor bowling, underperforming stars and a side that never truly found its identity. From overdependence on Jasprit Bumrah to tactical confusion and flat body language, MI's campaign exposed deeper cracks than just results.

Barcelona consider legal action after Florentino Perez's corruption claimsBarcelona are considering legal action against Real Madrid president Florentino Perez after he accused the Catalan giants of corruption during a fiery and wide-ranging press conference on Tuesday, escalating tensions between Spain's two biggest clubs.

Forget big-hitting, Gujarat Titans show how teams can dominate IPL with the ballIPL 2026: Gujarat Titans have pushed themselves to the edge of the playoffs with a disciplined bowling-first model. In an IPL shaped by batting excess, their rise has come through control, hard lengths and clear thinking.

Survive 2 days on Rs 125: Indian women footballers speak out on travel ordealIndian women footballers have alleged to be given just Rs 125 for a two-day train journey, with no official accompanying them and some players travelling without confirmed tickets.

Sophie Molineux to lead Australia as Brown dropped for Women's T20 World CupAustralia have named Sophie Molineux to lead their Women's T20 World Cup squad after confirming the all-rounder is fit for the 2026 tournament in England, with selectors also springing a surprise by leaving out pace bowler Darcie Brown.

Nahid Rana breathes hope into Bangladesh's Test pace legacy. Protect himBAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Bangladesh's Nahid Rana has offered fresh hope of building a genuine fast-bowling legacy in Test cricket, an area where the Tigers have struggled for 26 years while largely being known as a spin-dominated side in the longest format.

SRH head coach stands by batters after hitting new low in loss vs GTDaniel Vettori backed Sunrisers Hyderabad's batting unit after their record-low 86 against Gujarat Titans in Ahmedabad. He called the collapse a one-off blip and said the side could bounce back quickly.

Why Bhuvneshwar Kumar is right to ignore India comeback hysteriaIPL 2026, RCB vs KKR: Bhuvneshwar Kumar's superb IPL season has naturally revived India comeback talk, but the veteran pacer is right to stay away from the noise and focus on bowling well, staying fit and enjoying his cricket without added pressure.

IPL 2026: RCB close in on playoffs, but revival-hungry KKR stand firmly in the wayRCB vs KKR, IPL 2026 Preview: RCB head into their clash against KKR sitting comfortably near the top of the table, but Virat Kohli's sudden dip in form and questions around Romario Shepherd have added fresh intrigue. KKR, meanwhile, are sweating over Varun Chakravarthy's fitness as their play-off push enters survival mode.

Chettan's day out: CSK's Sanju Samson plays beach cricket with locals in ChennaiSanju Samson joined CSK fans for a game of beach cricket in Chennai, and the video went viral. Samson spent quality time with the fans and created a bond with them.

Is Hardik Pandya really injured? MI captain dazzles in late night net sessionHardik Pandya posted videos of a late-night net session after missing two Mumbai Indians matches with back spasms. The MI captain showed no signs of injury and seems to have stepped his recovery from back spasms.

Preity Zinta, PBKS slam fake stories on team as social media scrutiny growsPreity Zinta and Punjab Kings have publicly denounced the false stories circulating about the team and its players, as social media scrutiny of the franchise continues to intensify. PBKS have suffered a sharp drop in form after their latest losses in IPL 2026.

IPL Play of the Day: Rabada, Siraj unleash powerplay hell to stun HyderabadIPL 2026, GT vs SRH: Kagiso Rabada and Mohammed Siraj rattled the Sunrisers Hyderabad on Tuesday, May 12 and helped Gujarat Titans win by 82 runs at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Jason Collins, former NBA player, dies at 47 after cancer battleFormer NBA player Jason Collins has died at 47 after battling glioblastoma, his family said. Collins was a trailblazer who made history in 2013 by coming out as gay while still an active player.

Ronaldo's title wait continues after Al Nassr's last-minute draw vs Al HilalAl Nassr conceded a stoppage-time equaliser to draw 1-1 with Al Hilal in a crucial title-race derby. The result left Cristiano Ronaldo stunned and kept the Saudi Pro League race alive.

Pat Cummins backs SRH top-order despite 86 all-out vs GTIPL 2026: Pat Cummins backed Sunrisers Hyderabad's batters after their 82-run loss to Gujarat Titans in Ahmedabad. He said such collapses can happen in T20 cricket and praised Gujarat's near-perfect bowling.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
